name: FIBRODYSPLASIA OSSIFICANS PROGRESSIVA
definition: MYOSITIS OSSIFICANS PROGRESSIVA (misnomer since primarily connective tissues are rare slowly progressive sporadic / autosomal dominant disease with variable penetrance usually only one large phalanx present / synostosis of metacarpal + proximal phalanx

demographics.Histo: edema with proliferating fibroblasts in a loose myxoid matrix; subsequent collagen

dominant_finding: microdactyly of big toes (90%) and thumbs (50%)
distribution: progressive fusion of posterior arches of cervical spine
discriminating_features: rounded / linear calcification in neck / shoulders, para-vertebral region, hips, proximal
ossification of voluntary muscles, complete by 20-25 years (sparing of sphincters + head)
microdactyly of big toes (90%) and thumbs (50%)
phalangeal shortening of hand + foot (middle phalanx of 5th digit)

general: rounded / linear calcification in neck / shoulders, para-vertebral region, hips, proximal
ossification of voluntary muscles, complete by 20-25 years (sparing of sphincters + head)
microdactyly of big toes (90%) and thumbs (50%)
phalangeal shortening of hand + foot (middle phalanx of 5th digit)
thickening of medial cortex of tibia
progressive fusion of posterior arches of cervical spine
narrowed AP diameter of cervical + lumbar vertebral bodies
